sourceScope.sourceReference: American Academy of Actuaries practice note, May 2021
sourceScope.sourceStatusNote: Non-binding practice note / companion guidance; not authoritative regulatory text.

observedSectionWindows.note: Front matter, introduction, experience studies, morbidity, and assumption-setting opener.
Persistency, mortality, lapse, benefit exhaustion, anti-selection, and investment-return / discounting methods.
Financial reporting, valuation, reserve calculations, incurred claim reserve discussion, and first-principles / conversion closeout.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single practice note file.
Do not widen into the other Practice Notes files in the same folder.
Keep VM-20, VM-21, VM-22, VM-30, VM-31, ASOP 5, ASOP 18, ASOP 23, and FASB ASC 944-40 references review-only unless the same batch intentionally includes the referenced operational text.
Keep the disclaimer and the non-binding status visible as companion-guidance context, not as authoritative regulatory text.
Use page locators as the primary citation anchor; line references are not expected for this PDF slice.
exclusions: No full-document processing outside this source file.
No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other Practice Notes files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Front matter, introduction, experience studies, morbidity, and assumption-setting opener

topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the core assumption-setting and persistency material together as one narrow review-only slice.

topicMap.boundaryNote: Close the note with the financial reporting and valuation material while keeping the end boundary explicit.

ragReadyCriteria: review status no longer requires human disposition

validationImplications.portabilityNote: Keep the plan separate from VM-20, VM-21, VM-22, and the other Practice Notes files so the processor stays portable across source families.

operatingNote: This plan is intentionally conservative. It is a boundary-control artifact that should reduce review burden before any controlled LTCI practice-note extraction run begins.
